_meta.licensing: Each entry's per-source license is in its `license` field and governs the archived raw bytes plus any verbatim source text quoted in `normalized_content` (e.g. `narrative_excerpt`, `declaration_text`). Those third-party excerpts remain under the source's own license (WHO CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO; Imperial restricted-use assumption pending primary license footer; Wikipedia CC BY-SA 4.0; US CDC public domain). The CC BY 4.0 license declared for this repo in LICENSES.md covers only the schema, structure, SHA-256 hashes, and my own annotations in this file, not quoted third-party text or extracted third-party tables. Confirm permissions before commercial reuse or redistribution of restricted publisher material.
_meta.scope: Live provenance registry for public sources for the BDBV 2026 outbreak. Entries with raw_archive_status=public_bytes ship one fetched-and-archived byte payload under data/bundibugyo-2026/raw/<sha256>. Entries with raw_archive_status=private_restricted_bytes retain URL, timestamp, hash, and extracted factual metadata but do not redistribute publisher bytes.
_meta.verification_workflow: For public_bytes entries, re-run shasum -a 256 against the raw file to verify. For private_restricted_bytes entries, the content_hash records the original retrieval hash but the raw payload is intentionally not redistributed.

normalized_content.capture_role: restricted raw dashboard provenance; superseded for source-review display by drc-moh-epidemie-dashboard-sitrep-008-graphql-2026-05-23 and for corridor source load by the reviewed SitRep 007 PDF cumulative table
normalized_content.interpretation_caveat: Structured table semantics are not yet pinned. Sitrep/008 values appear to be recent/new report values, while SitRep 007 values in the same payload appear cumulative. Do not promote headline counts without resolving daily-delta versus cumulative meaning.
normalized_content.query: epidemie(id: "ebola-bundibugyo-2026", idType: SLUG) { rapportsHebdomandaires(first: 52) { reportsFields { dateRapportage datePublication situationProvince { province { nom zoneSante { nom casConfirmes casSuspects deces } } } } } }

entries.publisher: Ministere de la Sante Publique, Hygiene et Prevoyance sociale, Democratic Republic of the Congo

normalized_content.date_field_caveat: date_rapportage and date_publication are the dashboard API fields. For linked PDFs, verify the printed report/publication dates in the PDF before final manifest ingest; the API and PDF label can differ.
normalized_content.interpretation_caveat: DRC MoH dashboard fields are official, but the GraphQL zone rows must be tied back to the report/PDF table before using them as headline cumulative counts. SitRep 007 exposes cumulative rows; the latest dashboard rows may represent daily/new values until the matching PDF or table label is verified.
normalized_content.query: query EpidemieDashboard { epidemie(id: "ebola-bundibugyo-2026", idType: SLUG) { name epidemiesFields { codeOms dateDebut statut souche } rapportsHebdomandaires(first: 52) { edges { node { slug title reportsFields { dateRapportage datePublication pdfOfficiel { node { mediaItemUrl } } situationProvince { province { nom zoneSante { nom casConfirmes casSuspects deces } } } } } } } } }
normalized_content.row_total_caveat: These are summed health-zone rows from the dashboard payload. They may exclude non-zone rows in the PDF, such as samples without forms, and must not be treated as headline cumulative totals without report-level review.

verification_provenance.evidence_summary: Compared per-zone rows against SitRep 007 PDF cumulative table; Bunia confirmed 15 (007 cumulative) versus 6 (008 row) versus 0 (009 row) is incompatible with cumulative semantics. SitRep 009 dashboard_aggregate.basis explicitly states the dashboard rows are per-bulletin, not cumulative.

entries.publisher: Ministere de la Sante Publique, Hygiene et Prevoyance sociale, Democratic Republic of the Congo

normalized_content.date_field_caveat: The dashboard API date fields for this linked PDF lag the printed PDF/report labels. The manifest date policy uses the PDF-visible report date 2026-05-21 and publication date 2026-05-22.
normalized_content.new_confirmed_delta_note: PDF prose/Table I reports 19 new confirmed cases; these are not cumulative source-load counts.
normalized_content.query: query EpidemieDashboard { epidemie(id: "ebola-bundibugyo-2026", idType: SLUG) { name epidemiesFields { codeOms dateDebut statut souche } rapportsHebdomandaires(first: 52) { edges { node { slug title reportsFields { dateRapportage datePublication pdfOfficiel { node { mediaItemUrl } } situationProvince { province { nom zoneSante { nom casConfirmes casSuspects deces } } } } } } } } }
normalized_content.row_total_caveat: Affected-health-zone rows sum to 79 confirmed cases. The cumulative PDF headline is 83 confirmed cases because it includes 4 confirmed samples without case-investigation forms (Echantillons sans fiche), which are retained as unallocated headline context.
normalized_content.table_semantics_evidence: PDF Table I is explicitly a new/delta table (nouveaux cas). PDF Table IV is a separate cumulative table for suspected cases, suspected deaths, confirmed cases, and contacts by health zone as of 21 May 2026.

explicit_bdbv_claims: PHEIC declared 17 May 2026 for BDBV disease in DRC and Uganda.
Outbreak described as active and ongoing in north-eastern Ituri Province with cross-border spread to Uganda.
No licensed vaccine or specific treatment exists for Bundibugyo virus disease; response relies on public health measures and supportive care.
WHO and partners are supporting surveillance, laboratory testing, IPC, contact tracing, case management, cross-border coordination, and readiness.
normalized_content.narrative_excerpt: WHO AFRO outbreak hub carries useful official BDBV context, geography, PHEIC, countermeasure, and cross-border readiness claims, but no current count tuple on the captured hub page.

normalized_content.release_gate_note: Official WHO AFRO context/geography/readiness hub only; do not use as a count endpoint or May 25 snapshot trigger.

headline_deaths_cod_uga_composition.composition_note: Review endpoint composes 246 DRC suspected deaths from this INRB/INSP/UMIE release with one Uganda confirmed death from ecdc-bdbv-drc-uga-2026-05-27-live. Uganda suspected deaths are not published; the composition is disclosed as mixed status classes.

normalized_content.model_use: primary DRC authority/partner data release for national count promotion and publication-clock routing; DRC-only national fields are preserved as country-specific values, and COD+UGA deaths require an explicit Uganda component.
normalized_content.release_body: - Updated INSP Sitrep data with the new version of Sitrep 12 (Updated national suspected deaths)
normalized_content.release_gate_note: May advance the publication-state review route and DRC national headline components because it is a dated authority/partner release. It must not replace corridor source-load until health-zone table semantics are reviewed; COD+UGA deaths require explicit Uganda composition.
normalized_content.scope_caveat: This INRB/INSP/UMIE release is DRC-only. Do not collapse it into the COD+UGA headline without an explicit composition step that adds or reconciles Uganda on the same metric concept.

entries.publisher: Institut National de Recherche Biomedicale (INRB), Institut National de Sante Publique (INSP), and Unite de Modelisation et Intelligence Epidemique (UMIE)

normalized_content.count_source_label: On 26 May, the Ministry of Health in DRC reported a total of 121 confirmed cases (including 17 deaths) and 1 077 suspected cases (including 238 deaths) in Ituri, North Kivu, and South Kivu provinces. Uganda has reported seven confirmed cases, including one death.
normalized_content.date_semantics_caveat: The ECDC page is dated/as-of 2026-05-27 (og:updated_time + stamped 27 May 16:45). The count tuple is attributed to DRC MoH on 2026-05-26. Public trajectory points should use the source's data/reporting date (2026-05-26) for counts and retain the page publication/retrieval clock (2026-05-27) separately.
normalized_content.ecdc_assessment_quote: As of 27 May 2026, the outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus is still affecting the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, with increasing numbers of confirmed and suspected cases and deaths being reported.
normalized_content.metric_scope_caveat: ECDC 27 May separates DRC confirmed cases, DRC suspected cases, DRC confirmed deaths, DRC suspected deaths, and Uganda confirmed cases/confirmed deaths. Do not mix confirmed and suspected categories into a generic all-deaths endpoint without composition disclosure per NUMBERS_AUDIT Rule 3.
normalized_content.model_use: Primary regional cross-corroboration of the DRC MoH 26 May release; in concert with INRB build-2026-05-27 (DRC-only national_moh), supports promotion of DRC MoH 26 May to headline count endpoint for the May-27 publication-state snapshot. CDC May-26 retained as dated conflict anchor (105/906/10/223).
normalized_content.publication_title: Ebola virus disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
normalized_content.uganda_drc_travel_linkage_note: Three of the seven Uganda confirmed cases are linked to travel from DRC.

headline_deaths_cod_uga_composition.composition_note: Review endpoint composes 246 DRC suspected deaths from this INRB/INSP/UMIE release with one Uganda confirmed death from ecdc-bdbv-drc-uga-2026-05-27-live. Uganda suspected deaths are not published; the composition is disclosed as mixed status classes.

normalized_content.model_use: primary DRC authority/partner data release for national count promotion and publication-clock routing; DRC-only national fields are preserved as country-specific values, and COD+UGA deaths require an explicit Uganda component.
normalized_content.release_body:
- Healthzone level epi data up to 26th May from INSP (The public sitrep did not have this data)
- Testing capacity data from africa CDC
normalized_content.release_gate_note: May advance the publication-state review route and DRC national headline components because it is a dated authority/partner release. It must not replace corridor source-load until health-zone table semantics are reviewed; COD+UGA deaths require explicit Uganda composition.
normalized_content.scope_caveat: This INRB/INSP/UMIE release is DRC-only. Do not collapse it into the COD+UGA headline without an explicit composition step that adds or reconciles Uganda on the same metric concept.

normalized_content.uganda_anchor_refresh_note: Uganda anchor refreshed 7->15 (1 death unchanged) from CDC 2026-06-02 + ECDC 2026-06-03; prior 7 was the stale ECDC 2026-05-27 anchor. country-scope total 370->378.

normalized_content.format_drift_note: Builds from 2026-05-30 carry headerless CSVs with a UTF-8 BOM and an 'ND' missing-value sentinel; build-2026-06-01 also uses ISO dates. insp_per_zone_loader was made format-tolerant (utf-8-sig decode, header-optional positional reader, ND maps to 0) to ingest this build without breaking the older headered builds.
normalized_content.headline_count_status: per_zone_confirmed_promoted_for_attribution_at_2026-05-29; headline COD+UGA counts remain sourced from inrb-sitrep-017-2026-05-31

confirmed_cases.unmapped_new_zones_note: 22 INRB zones carry confirmed cases at 2026-05-29; 15 map to LOVS canonical ids (235 confirmed). The 7 newer zones (Beni, Mangala, Aungba, Gethy, Lita, Kyondo, Kalunguta; 8 confirmed) are not yet in the LOVS zone-alias bridge and remain in the unallocated residual pending a coordinated bridge plus map-geometry expansion.

normalized_content.model_use: primary DRC authority/partner per-zone release used for geographic confirmed-case attribution at 2026-05-29; the COD+UGA headline counts remain sourced from inrb-sitrep-017-2026-05-31 and this build's national rollup is not promoted to headline.
normalized_content.release_body: Updates to SitReps 15 and 16; new alias mapping plus detailed digitization decision logs.
normalized_content.release_gate_note: Advances the per-health-zone confirmed attribution layer to 2026-05-29 (22 zones upstream, 15 mapped to LOVS canonical ids). Must not replace the SitRep #017 headline; COD+UGA deaths require explicit Uganda composition. The cumulative suspected tier was retired 2026-06-02; only confirmed cases and confirmed deaths are ingested as cumulative metrics.
normalized_content.scope_caveat: This INRB/INSP/UMIE release is DRC-only. Do not collapse it into the COD+UGA headline without an explicit composition step that adds or reconciles Uganda on the same metric concept.

entries.publisher: Institut National de Recherche Biomedicale (INRB), Institut National de Sante Publique (INSP), and Unite de Modelisation et Intelligence Epidemique (UMIE)
US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

normalized_content.metric_scope_caveat: CDC current-situation case tiles are rendered client-side; the figures here are from rendered-page extraction (the raw HTML bytes are the JS shell). The Uganda count and probable tier are byte-corroborated by ECDC 2026-06-03 (ecdc-bdbv-drc-uga-2026-06-03-live), whose raw HTML carries the 15-confirmed sentence verbatim. CDC DRC figures (363 confirmed, 62 deaths) match INRB SitRep #019.
normalized_content.model_use: Uganda country-scope confirmed + probable anchor (Kampala/Wakiso). Official CDC. Updates the carried Uganda anchor on the country-scope composition only; does NOT update DRC MoH zone-attributed corridor source load.
normalized_content.narrative_excerpt: CDC Ebola Current Situation (data as of June 2, 2026): Uganda 15 confirmed cases, 1 confirmed death, 1 probable case, 1 probable death. DRC 363 confirmed cases, 62 confirmed deaths.

entries.publisher: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control

normalized_content.metric_scope_caveat: ECDC DRC figures (344 confirmed / 60 deaths, as of 1 Jun) lag the fresher INRB SitRep #019 DRC headline (363 / 62, 2 Jun); use INRB for DRC and this source for Uganda. The 15-confirmed sentence and the local-transmission sentence are present verbatim in the captured raw HTML (byte-verifiable).
normalized_content.model_use: Uganda confirmed anchor (byte-verifiable) + transmission characterization. ECDC states at least 7 of Uganda's cases are associated with local transmission events and 4 with travel links to DRC, per health officials.
normalized_content.narrative_excerpt: Uganda has reported a total of 15 confirmed cases, including one death. At least seven cases were associated with local transmission events and four with travel links to DRC, according to health officials.
